---
title: "AI in Research: Moving Beyond the Default"
subtitle: "Structuring Workflows from Simple Prompts to Agentic Systems"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    css: styles/KI4Forsking-reveal.css
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    embed-resources: true
---

## 

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](figures/pisa_hbsc_composite_scatter.png){width=100% fig-align="center"}
:::
::: {.column width="50%" style="font-size:0.5em;"}
```
Download hsbc data here: https://data-browser.hbsc.org/wp-content/uploads/csvs/data.csv  and meta data here https://data-browser.hbsc.org/wp-content/uploads/csvs/metadata.csv.
Then extract data for 15 year olds 
- who-5-well-being-index 
- life satisfaction
- self efficacy (2 measures)
from the data and calculate a composite score for each country.

Next go to https://zenodo.org/records/13382904 and download the PISA 2022 student file.
Build a composite score from reading, mathematics and science, and compute the average for each European country.

Finally, plot a scatterplot with the composite HBSC score on the x-axis and the composite PISA score on the y-axis. Label all the Nordic countries and highlight Norway and save the figure as figures/scatter_pisa_hsbc.png

Use R to do this.
Document all steps in a script, which you save as scripts/pisa_hbsc_scatter.
Save all data in the data folder.
```
:::

::::


## LLM problems for researcher

* LLMs are **prediction / interpolations machines**: They model the temporal order of tokens (words)
* This is **very useful**, because words that are correctly ordered do contain meaning
* But the way LLMS are built produces 2 problems for researchers when "new" chains of words are generated
  * **Regression to the mode** of the training data
  * **Hallucination**

As researchers, we want to harness the usefulness of LLMs while controlling their problems

---

## Solutions for LLM problems

* Use thinking mode^[Naming conventions depend on provider] by default (not fast)
* Good **custom instructions** and **prompts**
* Fill the **context windows** with relevant & accurate information
* Use tools with **inbuilt verification**
* **Don't ask LLMs to do work that you can't verify**^[Think of LLMs as multiplying the competences you have, not as giving you completely new competences.]

---

## Custom instructions

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
* All models nowadays allow to **change the personality** 
* Instructions for researchers
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
<div id="ci-wrap-instructions" style="width:100%;">
  <img src="figures/CustomInstructions1.png" style="width:100%;"/>
  <img src="figures/CustomInstructions2.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
  <img src="figures/CustomInstructions3.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
  <img src="figures/CustomInstructions4.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
</div>
<div id="ci-caption-instructions" style="text-align:center;font-size:0.8em;color:#555;">1 / 4</div>
<script>
(function(){
  var wrap = document.getElementById('ci-wrap-instructions');
  var slides = wrap.querySelectorAll('img');
  var cap = document.getElementById('ci-caption-instructions');
  var n = slides.length;
  var i = 0;
  setInterval(function(){
    slides[i].style.display = 'none';
    i = (i+1) % n;
    slides[i].style.display = 'block';
    cap.textContent = (i+1) + " / " + n;
  }, 3000);
})();
</script>
:::

::::

```
- Say "I don't know" rather than guessing; never fabricate citations or statistics
- Use web search and tools to verify facts before stating them
- Cite primary literature; prefer peer-reviewed sources over summaries
- Push back on flawed reasoning or unsupported claims
- Think step by step and check your reasoning before answering
- Read papers critically: flag when conclusions outrun the evidence, when study designs cannot support causal claims, or when limitations are understated
- Be concise; do not pad responses or ask unnecessary follow-up questions
- Do not suggest next steps or offer further help unless asked

```

## Custom chatbots

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
* Provide additional info about desired behavior for specific tasks, and use task specific chat bots 
* Methods consultant, copy editor english, grant reviewer, copy - editor "enkelt språk"
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
<div id="ci-wrap-chats" style="width:100%;">
  <img src="figures/CustomChats1.png" style="width:100%;"/>
  <img src="figures/CustomChats2.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
  <img src="figures/CustomChats3.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
</div>
<div id="ci-caption-chats" style="text-align:center;font-size:0.8em;color:#555;">1 / 3</div>
<script>
(function(){
  var wrap = document.getElementById('ci-wrap-chats');
  var slides = wrap.querySelectorAll('img');
  var cap = document.getElementById('ci-caption-chats');
  var n = slides.length;
  var i = 0;
  setInterval(function(){
    slides[i].style.display = 'none';
    i = (i+1) % n;
    slides[i].style.display = 'block';
    cap.textContent = (i+1) + " / " + n;
  }, 3000);
})();
</script>
:::

::::



Chatbot contents

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="30%"}
* Name
* Description
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
* **Instructions**
* **Knowledge**
:::

::: {.column width="30%"}
* Tools
:::

::::


## Retrieval Augmented Generation

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
* Allows to limit answers to bew derived from provided text
* Uses semantic search to find relevant text in a knowledge base
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
<div id="ci-wrap-notebooklm" style="width:100%;">
  <img src="figures/NotbookLM1.png" style="width:100%;"/>
  <img src="figures/NotbookLM2.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
  <img src="figures/NotbookLM3.png" style="width:100%;display:none;"/>
</div>
<div id="ci-caption-notebooklm" style="text-align:center;font-size:0.8em;color:#555;">1 / 3</div>
<script>
(function(){
  var wrap = document.getElementById('ci-wrap-notebooklm');
  var slides = wrap.querySelectorAll('img');
  var cap = document.getElementById('ci-caption-notebooklm');
  var n = slides.length;
  var i = 0;
  setInterval(function(){
    slides[i].style.display = 'none';
    i = (i+1) % n;
    slides[i].style.display = 'block';
    cap.textContent = (i+1) + " / " + n;
  }, 3000);
})();
</script>
:::
::::

  * **Elicit** (elicit.com, great but uses mostly abstracts :-()
  * **NotebookLM** (https://notebooklm.google.com/)
* Also here: Garbage in, garbage out. (`pdfParser` in R)




## AI-assisted text generation — with verification

* FHI produces reports for hundreds of municipalities, schools, ... — writing subgroup-specific text by hand doesn't scale
* LLMs can generate this text automatically from statistical tables
* Also useful for drafting results sections from analysis output
* Risk: LLMs can hallucinate numbers —> **programmatic verification** 
* In R: `tblscribe` ^[compares numbers in generated text against source table]

## Agentic systems

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="60%"}
Whats that? 

  * Tools that write and execute code.
  * Work iteratively — run code, read errors, and self-correct.
  * Most effective when you can judge whether the output is correct.
  
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
Example tools:

  * Cursor (cursor.com)
  * Claude Code/Workspace (claude.com)
  * Copilot (for Office files)
  * ...

:::

::::

## Summary

* LLMs are extremly powerful, but also fallible
* Need to put in guardrails: Custom instructions, Custom Chatbots, RAGs, Programmatic verification
* Agentic systems are the next step: Tools that write and execute code
* We are living the future now: The question is not if LLMs will change how we do research, but how.
